mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, so S&P 500 forward returns turn positive when policy stays below neutral and does not rise over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the sign relation is negative in more than 60% of easing observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals worsening household income and slower risk appetite, so equities underperform over the next month after an unemployment upturn becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average forward return is not below unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal consumer spending, which reduces revenue expectations and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if declining RSAFS does not coincide with weaker 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the direction is positive in a rolling 3-year window with statistically material frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO together with a decline in UNRATE indicates improving real activity and labor utilization, which boosts earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if combined INDPRO up and UNRATE down signals do not outperform baseline over 60 days, or if the joint signal is no better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rebounds from a depressed level, the housing cycle improves demand for materials, labor, and credit-sensitive sectors, leading to higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by at least 5% month over month after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts rebounds are not followed by positive 20-day equity returns more often than not, or if the mean forward return is not significantly above zero after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising CPIAUCSL with an unchanged FEDFUNDS rate creates negative real policy rates and eases financial conditions, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up / policy-flat episodes do not produce positive 20-day returns, or if higher CPI with flat FEDFUNDS is associated with negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in PAYEMS signals sustained payroll growth and higher aggregate income, which strengthens equity earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 0.2% over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll acceleration does not predict higher 60-day returns, or if positive payroll bursts are followed by below-average equity returns across a rolling 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS confirms that both real output and nominal sales are accelerating, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because earnings expectations rise together with demand.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales improvements do not outperform baseline over 20 days, or if the combined signal fails to beat either proxy individually.
